document.title: TEST REPORT
authorityOrJurisdiction: Bureau Veritas Korea Co., Ltd. Consumer Product Services

sourceSummary.oneLine: Bureau Veritas CPS Korea test report for #4920 covering restricted substances, including heavy metals, phthalates, and organotin content.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports restricted substance compliance evidence for submitted sample #4920 and listed item or part numbers.
keyPoints: Wet chemical analysis for restricted substances.
Antimony(Sb) and Beryllium(Be) results were ND.
Listed phthalates tested with reference to IEC 62321-8: 2017 were ND.
Organotin testing reported TBT ND, TPT ND, DBT 1 mg/kg, and DOT ND.

entities.name: Bureau Veritas CPS Korea
entities.evidence: Bureau Veritas CPS Korea RM409, 8F RM901~902, RM908~915 (O-Biz tower) 126, Beolmal-ro, Dongan-gu, Anyang-si, Gyeonggi-do, 14057, Republic of Korea

entities.name: Bureau Veritas Korea Co., Ltd. Consumer Product Services
entities.evidence: Authorized by Bureau Veritas Korea Co., Ltd. Consumer Product Services

entities.name: JIN KIM
entities.evidence: JIN KIM Lab Manager / Bureau Veritas Korea CPS

entities.name: European Parliament and Council Directive 2011/65/EU
entities.evidence: List of Phthalates [ European Parliament and Council Directive 2011/65/EU ]

concepts.name: Restricted substances wet chemical analysis
concepts.evidence: Test Type : Wet chemical analysis for restricted substances.

concepts.name: KOLAS recognition
concepts.evidence: This report is independent of the KOLAS recognition criteria (KS Q ISO/IEC 17025) and KOLAS recognition.

evidence.claim: The document is a test report issued on 2024. 04. 12 with report number (8224)690-0058-001.
evidence.evidenceText: TEST REPORT Report No. : (8224)690-0058-001 Date Issued : 2024. 04. 12 Page 1 of 9

evidence.claim: The submitted product name or type is #4920.
The item or part numbers listed are 4914, 4920, 4930, 4930F, 4929, 4949, 4955, 4959, and 4959F.
The company name is 3M Korea and the manufacturer/vendor is 3M Korea Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Company Name : 3M Korea ... Manufacturer/Vendor : 3M Korea Ltd.

evidence.claim: The sample was received on 2024. 04. 04 and tested from 2024. 04. 04 to 2024. 04. 12.
The test type was wet chemical analysis for restricted substances.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Type : Wet chemical analysis for restricted substances.

evidence.claim: Heavy metals testing for Antimony(Sb) and Beryllium(Be) used reference to US EPA3052 and ICP-OES, with both results ND.
evidence.evidenceText: Antimony(Sb) With reference to US EPA3052, Determined by ICP-OES. mg/kg 2 ND Beryllium(Be) mg/kg 2 ND

evidence.claim: Phthalates testing used reference to IEC 62321-8: 2017 and GC-MS & LC-MS, with listed results ND.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to IEC 62321-8: 2017, Determined by GC-MS & LC-MS. mg/kg 50 ND

evidence.claim: The report lists phthalates under European Parliament and Council Directive 2011/65/EU.
evidence.evidenceText: List of Phthalates [ European Parliament and Council Directive 2011/65/EU ]

evidence.claim: Organotin testing used reference to ISO 17353:2004(Mod) and GC/MS.
evidence.evidenceText: With reference to ISO 17353:2004(Mod), determined by GC/MS.

evidence.claim: Organotin content results were Tributyl Monoethyl tin (TBT) ND, Triphenyl Monoethyl tin (TPT) ND, Dibutyl Diethyl tin (DBT) 1 mg/kg, and Dioctyl Diethyl tin (DOT) ND.
evidence.evidenceText: Tributyl Monoethyl tin (TBT) ... mg/kg 1 ND Triphenyl Monoethyl tin (TPT) mg/kg 1 ND Dibutyl Diethyl tin (DBT) mg/kg 1 1 Dioctyl Diethyl tin (DOT) mg/kg 1 ND

evidence.claim: The report states it is independent of KOLAS recognition criteria and KOLAS recognition.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is independent of the KOLAS recognition criteria (KS Q ISO/IEC 17025) and KOLAS recognition.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit revision or version number for the report was present; BVCPS-QPF-24-07(04) appears to be a form identifier and was not treated as the report revision.
The source is a test report from a laboratory and references Directive 2011/65/EU, but it does not state a formal regulatory compliance conclusion or certificate status.
The report states it is independent of KOLAS recognition criteria and KOLAS recognition, so accreditation status should not be inferred.
